segments.text: Yahweh said to me,
Take a large tablet, and write on it with a man’s pen, ‘For Maher Shalal Hash Baz’;
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: and I will take for myself faithful witnesses to testify: Uriah the priest, and Zechariah the son of Jeberechiah.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: I went to the prophetess, and she conceived, and bore a son. Then Yahweh said to me,
Call his name ‘Maher Shalal Hash Baz.’
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: For before the child knows how to say, ‘My father’ and ‘My mother,’ the riches of Damascus and the plunder of Samaria will be carried away by the king of Assyria.
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: Yahweh spoke to me yet again, saying,
Because this people has refused the waters of Shiloah that go softly, and rejoice in Rezin and Remaliah’s son;
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: now therefore, behold, the Lord brings upon them the mighty flood waters of the River: the king of Assyria and all his glory. It will come up over all its channels, and go over all its banks.
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: It will sweep onward into Judah. It will overflow and pass through. It will reach even to the neck. The stretching out of its wings will fill the width of your land, O Immanuel.
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: Make an uproar, you peoples, and be broken in pieces! Listen, all you from far countries: dress for battle, and be shattered! Dress for battle, and be shattered!
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: Take counsel together, and it will be brought to nothing; speak the word, and it will not st and, for God is with us.
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: For Yahweh spoke this to me with a strong hand, and instructed me not to walk in the way of this people, saying,
Don’t call a conspiracy all that this people call a conspiracy. Don’t fear their threats or be terrorized.
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: Yahweh of Armies is who you must respect as holy. He is the one you must fear. He is the one you must dread.
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: He will be a sanctuary, but for both houses of Israel, he will be a stumbling stone and a rock that makes them fall. For the people of Jerusalem, he will be a trap and a snare.
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: Many will stumble over it, fall, be broken, be snared, and be captured.
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: Wrap up the covenant. Seal the law among my disciples.
I will wait for Yahweh, who hides his face from the house of Jacob, and I will look for him.
Behold, I and the children whom Yahweh has given me are for signs and for wonders in Israel from Yahweh of Armies, who dwells in Mount Zion.
When they tell you,
Consult with those who have familiar spirits and with the wizards, who chirp and who mutter,
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: shouldn’t a people consult with their God? Should they consult the dead on behalf of the living?
Turn to the law and to the covenant! If they don’t speak according to this word, surely there is no morning for them.
They will pass through it, very distressed and hungry. It will happen that when they are hungry, they will worry, and curse their king and their God. They will turn their faces upward,
then look to the earth and see distress, darkness, and the gloom of anguish. They will be driven into thick darkness.
